card.intuition: dp[j] = True if some subset of processed items sums to j. For each item, iterate j from target down to item value: dp[j] |= dp[j - item]. Iterating backwards prevents using the same item twice (0/1 constraint).
card.analogy: Checking whether a group of friends can split a restaurant bill exactly: try every combination of who contributes, keeping track of every reachable total. Each new person either joins or doesn't.
when_to_use: NP-hard in general, but pseudo-polynomial with DP when target is bounded
gotchas: Iterate j from target DOWN to num — going up would allow reusing items (unbounded knapsack behaviour)
dp is a boolean array; alternatively use a Python set of reachable sums
Bitset optimisation: represent dp as a Python int and use bitwise OR with shifts for O(n · target/64)

exercise.prompt: Implement subset sum using 1D boolean DP.
hints: dp[j] becomes True if dp[j] was already True OR if dp[j - num] is True (we include num in the subset)
exercise.test_code:
assert subset_sum([3,34,4,12,5,2], 9)  == True
assert subset_sum([3,34,4,12,5,2], 30) == False
assert subset_sum([], 0)                == True
assert subset_sum([], 1)                == False
assert subset_sum([1,2,3,4,5], 15)      == True   # sum of all
assert subset_sum([1,2,3,4,5], 16)      == False
print('PASS')
